# terrible_theforeman_foreman_activation_key (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `name` (String) Name of the activation key

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `auto_attach` (Boolean) Set Auto-Attach on or off The `auto_attach` parameter is deprecated since Katello 4.13 and not available after Katello 4.19
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `content_overrides` (String) List of content overrides that include label and override state Label refers to repository `content_label`. For Red Hat products for example `rhel-7-server-rpms`. For custom products it's in the format `<organization_label>_<product_label>_<repository_label>`, e.g. `ExampleOrg_ExampleProduct_ExampleRepository`. Override state ('enabled', 'disabled', or 'default') sets initial state of repository for newly registered hosts
- `content_view` (String) Name of the content view
- `content_view_environments` (String) List of content view environments to add to activation key. Mutually exclusive with `content_view` and `lifecycle_environment`.
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `description` (String) Description of the activation key
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of nameâ†’value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `host_collections` (String) List of host collections to add to activation key
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `lifecycle_environment` (String) Name of the lifecycle environment
- `max_hosts` (Number) Maximum number of registered content hosts. Required if *unlimited_hosts=false*
- `new_name` (String) Name of the new activation key when state == copied
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `purpose_addons` (String) Sets the system purpose add-ons
- `purpose_role` (String) Sets the system purpose role
- `purpose_usage` (String) Sets the system purpose usage
- `release_version` (String) Set the content release version
- `service_level` (String) Set the service level
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `state` (String) State of the Activation Key If `copied` the key will be copied to a new one with *new_name* as the name and all other fields left untouched `present_with_defaults` will ensure the entity exists, but won't update existing ones
- `subscriptions` (String) List of subscriptions that include either Name, Pool ID, or Upstream Pool ID. Pool IDs are preferred since Names and Upstream Pool IDs are not guaranteed to be unique. The module will fail if it finds more than one match. This parameter is not supported in SCA mode.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `unlimited_hosts` (Boolean) Can the activation key have unlimited hosts

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `entity` (String) Final state of the affected entities grouped by their type.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
